## Chronology Spine

## Core Truth Set
- (4/5) The Yatasi are framed as a close variation of the Natchitoches baseline rather than a completely separate system. | Quote: "The Yatasi are not a completely different world. They are a close variation of the Natchitoches baseline."
- (4/5) Yatasi trade behavior is framed as participatory and dependency-oriented rather than central node control. | Quote: "They are not driving trade. They are participating within it."
- (4/5) Diplomacy is portrayed as personal-trust-first and relationship-driven rather than large-scale strategic positioning. | Quote: "More intimate, relationship-driven diplomacy... less strategic positioning in large-scale alliances."
- (3/5) The profile emphasizes weakened or fragmented settlement continuity, with greater vulnerability to external pressure. | Quote: "Historically weakened and reduced over time... more fragmented settlement identity."
- (3/5) Language variation is framed as subtle and insider-legible (tone/expression/cadence) despite close dialect proximity. | Quote: "Likely very close dialectically... subtle differences in tone, expression, cadence."

## Setting Anchors
- Yatasi Interior Tributary Zone [REGION]: Interior-facing habitation and movement space less exposed to main-artery traffic and visibility pressures.

## Continuity Constraints
- Personal Trust Before Negotiation: Diplomatic sequencing constraint.
- Relationship Entry Over Traffic Entry: Access protocol constraint for Yatasi-facing scenes.
- Stabilize Access, Not Trade Domination: Trade-governance constraint for difference profile.

## Open Research Gaps
- What dated milestones best map Yatasi settlement weakening and merger dynamics with neighboring groups? (priority 2): Needed to avoid compressing multi-decade structural changes into a single narrative beat.
- What linguistic markers (tone, cadence, phrase choices) can be safely treated as Yatasi-distinct in dialogue? (priority 2): Needed for voice differentiation that avoids generic or over-assertive language claims.
- Which concrete exchange dependencies defined Yatasi trade participation under Natchitoches-adjacent systems? (priority 2): Needed to model stabilization scenes with credible economic specifics.

## Next Chapter Assembly Rule
- Do not promote a chapter beat to the core story unless it references at least one core claim, one chronology anchor (or explicit open gap), and one continuity constraint.
